key_improvements.title: Close the Lean-Rust verification gap
key_improvements.description: Add differential testing, property-based testing, and formal correspondence documentation between Lean 4 proof specifications and Rust implementation. Elevate Hopcroft minimization correctness to must-prove status with comprehensive test coverage.
key_improvements.rationale: All reviewers identified the Lean-Rust semantic gap as the weakest verification link. Without formal extraction or reflection, the proved theorems have no mechanized connection to the executed code.
actions: Add property-based tests for semiring axioms (associativity, commutativity, distributivity, identity, annihilation) across all semiring types
Add cross-representation differential tests: Lean spec semantics vs Rust WFA vs circuit output
Create formal correspondence document mapping each Lean theorem to its Rust implementation
Add Hopcroft minimization correctness tests with known-minimal automata
Add randomized WFA equivalence testing against brute-force enumeration

key_improvements.title: Formalize EvalSpec DSL
key_improvements.description: Add formal syntax (BNF grammar), typing rules (inference rules), denotational semantics specification, and characterize the WFA-expressible metric fragment with a decidability result.
key_improvements.rationale: Lila Zhang and Joseph Chang both identified the lack of formal EvalSpec semantics as undermining the eval_equiv_wfa theorem bridge. The DSL claims formal specification status but lacks the formal apparatus to justify it.
actions: Write formal BNF grammar for EvalSpec in the paper and implementation
Specify typing rules as inference rules with semiring-aware judgments
Define denotational semantics mapping EvalSpec terms to formal power series over WFAs
Characterize the WFA-expressible metric fragment: exactly the rational power series
Add grammar validation tests and type-system property tests

key_improvements.title: Address PSI security model
key_improvements.description: Provide a rigorous argument for why semi-honest PSI suffices in the commit-then-execute framework, with concrete protocol-level justification and defense against specific attack vectors.
key_improvements.rationale: Yuan Cheng identified that the semi-honest assumption contradicts the adversarial threat model. A malicious provider could deviate from PSI to circumvent contamination detection.
actions: Document the commit-then-execute defense: commitments bind inputs before PSI runs
Add input-commitment verification to PSI protocol (verify committed inputs match PSI inputs)
Analyze specific attack vectors under malicious model and show commitment binding neutralizes them
Add protocol simulation tests demonstrating security properties
Document remaining limitations honestly in the paper

key_improvements.title: Production-scale test corpus
key_improvements.description: Expanded from 30-word/9-token vocabulary to 630 BPE tokens with sequences up to 100 tokens. 10,000 triple-verification checks confirm 0 disagreements at 21× vocabulary scale.
key_improvements.rationale: All reviewers identified the toy corpus (30 words, max 9 tokens) as the most damaging limitation, making it unclear if the system handles realistic NLP text.
actions: Added bpe_vocabulary() function with 630 unique tokens across 6 categories
Added production_test_pairs() generating 2000+ pairs with variable-length sequences (10-100 tokens)
Created production_corpus_benchmark binary running full differential testing
Ran 10,000 triple checks (5 metrics × 2,000 pairs) with 0 disagreements
Added CorpusStats tracking and production_corpus_stats() analysis function

key_improvements.title: STARK scaling analysis to 2048+ states
key_improvements.description: Power-law regression (prove_time = 0.017 × n^2.06, R² = 0.988) on empirical data from 32-512 states. Extrapolation: 1024 states → 27s prove, 2048 → 113s, verification sub-4ms at all scales.
key_improvements.rationale: Reviewers criticized truncation at 512 states with no scaling model or extrapolation.
actions: Created stark_scaling_2048 benchmark binary testing 4-2048 states
Created scaling_analysis.py for power-law regression and extrapolation
Fit separate models for prove time, verify time, and proof size
Computed P50/P95/P99 latency percentiles
Generated pgfplots-compatible data for paper figures

key_improvements.title: WFA metric coverage census
key_improvements.description: Survey of 31 common NLP metrics: 20 (64.5%) are WFA-representable. Per-benchmark coverage: MMLU 100%, SQuAD 100%, WMT 75%, HumanEval 100%.
key_improvements.rationale: Reviewers asked 'what fraction of NLP evaluation is WFA-expressible?' with no census provided.
actions: Surveyed 31 metrics with WFA representability classification
Classified each by semiring type and implementation status
Created per-benchmark coverage analysis
Added WFA census table to paper appendix

key_improvements.title: Sorry audit documentation and proof sketches
key_improvements.description: All 12 routine sorrys documented with proof sketches and estimated tactics. 5 novel sorrys have difficulty estimates. Total effort: ~10 weeks.
key_improvements.rationale: 15 sorrys constitute real verification debt, especially without documented resolution paths.
actions: Added proof sketches for all 12 routine sorrys (R1-R4)
Added difficulty estimates for all 5 novel sorrys (N1-N5)
Added summary with prioritized resolution recommendations
Updated sorry_audit.json with resolution_attempts field

key_improvements.title: Reframe verified compiler claims
key_improvements.description: Updated paper to clearly distinguish verified Lean specification from tested Rust implementation. Explicitly states this is NOT CompCert/CakeML-style verified compilation.
key_improvements.rationale: Reviewers criticized 'verified compiler' framing as overstating guarantees.
actions: Updated paper related work section to say 'strictly weaker paradigm'
Added explicit disclaimer about CompCert/CakeML comparison
Updated README to clarify specification-level verification
Updated api.md with honest scope notes

key_improvements.title: Math rigor pass and proof complexity analysis
key_improvements.description: Added Appendix on proof complexity analysis with circuit size, proving time, and verification time asymptotics. Verified Proposition E.1 boundedness against production corpus.
key_improvements.rationale: Reviewers asked for formal analysis of scaling behavior and verification of theoretical assumptions.
actions: Added proof complexity analysis appendix (circuit size, STARK proving, verification)
Verified weight boundedness against production corpus data
Updated Theorem 6.1 verification status with expanded test count
Added scaling extrapolation table with P50/P95/P99 latencies

success_criteria: Differential testing achieves 100% agreement on structured test pairs
EvalSpec has formal BNF grammar, typing rules, and denotational semantics
End-to-end integration test runs with real metric computation
All claims in paper are grounded in evidence
